summary: Iteration 4 auth testing for EdilControl. 25/26 backend auth tests PASSED. JWT login, token verification, protected middleware, bcrypt hashing, seed idempotency all working. Frontend login → dashboard → AI chat → logout full flow works perfectly. One real backend bug found in brute force lockout logic: returns 500 instead of 429 due to naive/aware datetime mismatch, and identifier uses request.client.host which varies across ingress pods so counter never accumulates to 5 in production.

critical.issue: Brute force lockout returns HTTP 500 instead of 429 after 5 failed attempts. Root cause: _check_lockout() compares `datetime.now(timezone.utc)` (offset-aware) with `last_attempt` read from MongoDB (naive) → `TypeError: can't subtract offset-naive and offset-aware datetimes`. Fix: normalize `last = last.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc) if last.tzinfo is None else last` before subtracting, OR store as ISO string.

Brute force identifier uses `request.client.host` which, behind k8s ingress with multiple backend pods, returns a DIFFERENT internal proxy IP per request (observed 10.81.2.194 and 10.81.7.199 for same real client). Counter never accumulates → lockout never triggers in production. Fix: read `X-Forwarded-For` (first IP) or `X-Real-IP` header when present.

action_items: Fix _check_lockout datetime comparison: ensure `last_attempt` is timezone-aware before subtraction (store as ISO string or call .replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc) on read).
Fix brute force IP detection behind ingress: use X-Forwarded-For / X-Real-IP instead of request.client.host, otherwise lockout never fires in production.
After applying fixes, clear db.login_attempts and re-run test_auth.py::TestBruteForce to validate 429 response.
critical_code_review_comments: Line ~805 auth_middleware duplicates JWT decode logic from get_current_user → DRY violation (two places to maintain).
Line ~109 _check_lockout: datetime TZ bug (described above). Same code also silently catches bcrypt exceptions — acceptable for verify_password but masks config issues.
server.py is 842 lines — past the 700-line threshold. Consider splitting into auth.py / cantieri.py / ai_advisor.py modules.

PUBLIC_PATHS whitelist is fine, but middleware is applied AFTER CORSMiddleware via app.add_middleware — ordering is correct since FastAPI executes middleware in reverse order of registration. Worth adding a code comment.
login endpoint does not sanitize/trim username; case-insensitive handling only happens in identifier but DB lookup is case-sensitive (`{'username': req.username}`) — minor inconsistency.

success_rate.backend: 96% (25/26 auth tests; single failure is a real backend bug not a test issue)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (login, redirect, logout, dashboard KPIs, AI chat post-login all working)

seed_data_creation: None created; /api/seed-demo already populated DB in previous iterations.
context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent fixes the two brute force bugs, re-run `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_auth.py::TestBruteForce -v` to confirm attempt #6 returns 429 (not 500). Also verify behind k8s ingress by running the full test file against REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. Note: test_auth.py clears login_attempts collection before each login-related test to avoid cross-test pollution. Existing test_edilcontrol_api.py and test_ai_advisor.py from prior iterations do NOT include auth headers; they will now all fail with 401 — either update them with an auth fixture or leave as-is (auth tests cover the happy path).

rca of the issue.bug: Brute force lockout returns 500 instead of 429
rca of the issue.reproduction: 1) Clear db.login_attempts. 2) POST /api/auth/login with wrong password 5 times. 3) Attempt #6 returns 500 Internal Server Error instead of 429 lockout.
rca of the issue.root_cause: In _check_lockout (server.py line 109), `last = rec.get('last_attempt')` returns a naive datetime (MongoDB drops tzinfo on insert when using datetime.now(timezone.utc) unless codec_options specify tz_aware=True). Subtracting this from `datetime.now(timezone.utc)` (aware) raises TypeError, which FastAPI converts to 500.
rca of the issue.mitigation: Either: (a) normalize on read: `if last and last.tzinfo is None: last = last.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)`; (b) configure motor client with `tz_aware=True`; or (c) store last_attempt as ISO string and parse with fromisoformat.
